Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves that turn dark brown/black with yellow halo; spots on fruit are raised and scab-like.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides. Remove and destroy infected plant debris. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds, practice crop rotation (2-3 years), avoid working in fields when leaves are wet.

Pepper__bell___healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring, balanced fertilization, and proper irrigation.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown concentric ring spots (target-board pattern) on older leaves, yellowing around lesions.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or mancozeb. Remove infected leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops, avoid overhead watering, ensure good plant spacing for airflow, use resistant varieties.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked pale green to black lesions on leaves, white fungal growth on undersides in humid conditions, rapid plant collapse.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides (metalaxyl, mancozeb) immediately. Destroy infected plants to stop spread.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant certified disease-free tubers, avoid excess irrigation, monitor closely during cool wet weather.

Potato___healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring and balanced fertilization.

Tomato_Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small dark, greasy-looking spots on leaves and fruit, leaves may yellow and drop.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based sprays. Remove severely infected plants.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use disease-free seed, avoid overhead irrigation, rotate crops.

Tomato_Early_blight.symptoms: Concentric dark rings on lower/older leaves, yellowing around lesions, stem lesions possible.
Tomato_Early_blight.prevention: Mulch soil, avoid wetting leaves, rotate crops, stake plants for airflow.

Tomato_Late_blight.symptoms: Large irregular water-soaked lesions on leaves, white mold on undersides, rapid browning and plant death.
Tomato_Late_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides immediately (metalaxyl-based), remove and destroy infected plants.
Tomato_Late_blight.prevention: Avoid overhead watering, ensure good drainage/airflow, plant resistant varieties.

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale yellow spots on upper leaf surface, olive-green to grayish mold on undersides.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.treatment: Improve ventilation, apply fungicide if severe, remove infected leaves.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.prevention: Reduce humidity in greenhouse/field, avoid leaf wetness, ensure spacing.

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Small circular spots with dark borders and light gray centers, mainly on lower leaves.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Apply fungicide, remove infected lower leaves, avoid overhead watering.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Crop rotation, mulching, remove plant debris after harvest.

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.symptoms: Fine yellow stippling on leaves, fine webbing visible, leaves turn bronze/dry in severe cases.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.treatment: Apply miticide or insecticidal soap, increase humidity, introduce predatory mites.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.prevention: Avoid drought stress, regularly hose down plants, monitor early in hot dry weather.

Tomato__Target_Spot.symptoms: Brown circular lesions with concentric rings on leaves and fruit.
Tomato__Target_Spot.prevention: Crop rotation, avoid leaf wetness, proper spacing.

Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of leaves, stunted growth, reduced fruit yield.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.treatment: No cure once infected - remove infected plants. Control whitefly vector with insecticides/sticky traps.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.prevention: Use virus-resistant varieties, control whiteflies, use reflective mulch, remove weeds nearby.

Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mottled light/dark green pattern on leaves, leaf curling, stunted plant growth.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: No chemical cure - remove and destroy infected plants immediately.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Disinfect tools between plants, avoid tobacco use near plants, use resistant varieties.

Tomato_healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring, balanced fertilization, and proper irrigation.

_default.symptoms: Visual symptoms detected on the leaf that don't exactly match our known database entries. Please consult a local agricultural expert for confirmation.
_default.treatment: Isolate the affected plant if possible. Consider a broad-spectrum fungicide/bactericide after expert confirmation.
_default.prevention: Maintain crop rotation, proper spacing, avoid leaf wetness, and monitor plants regularly.
